name: apple-mail
description: Comprehensive guide for accessing and automating Apple Mail using AppleScript, including reading emails, sending messages, and managing mailboxes.
capabilities: List all configured mail accounts in Apple Mail
Read emails with full metadata from any account inbox
Send new emails programmatically with custom subject and body
Reply to emails while preserving thread context
Search emails by sender, subject, or content
Move, archive, or delete emails based on criteria
author: BfdCampos
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing AppleScript examples for Apple Mail automation. No executable code, no network calls, no file system modifications. All functionality requires user to manually execute osascript commands. Static findings are false positives from scanner misinterpreting documentation syntax (heredocs, AppleScript keywords) as security patterns.
valueStatement: Manually searching, reading, and sending emails wastes time. This skill provides ready-to-use AppleScript examples so you can automate email workflows directly from Claude. Read, send, reply, search, and manage Apple Mail without switching apps.
limitations: Works only with Apple Mail on macOS
Requires macOS privacy permission for Mail access
Does not support Gmail, Outlook, or other email clients
Cannot access encrypted or password-protected mailboxes

useCases.title: Automated code review notifications
useCases.description: Send automated emails to teammates when code is ready for review or CI tests complete.

useCases.title: Batch email processing
useCases.description: Search and archive emails from specific senders or with certain keywords automatically.

useCases.title: Unread email monitoring
useCases.description: Check unread message counts and get summaries of recent emails without opening Mail.

promptTemplates.title: List mail accounts
promptTemplates.prompt: List all the email accounts configured in my Apple Mail.

promptTemplates.title: Read recent emails
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me the last 5 emails from my Gmail inbox in Apple Mail.

promptTemplates.title: Send email
promptTemplates.prompt: Send an email to team@example.com with subject 'Weekly Update' and body summarising my progress today.

promptTemplates.title: Archive by sender
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all emails from newsletter@example.com and move them to the Archive mailbox.

bestPractices: Test scripts with visible:true before using visible:false to avoid sending emails unexpectedly
Always specify the exact account name rather than relying on email address matching
Use the osascript heredoc syntax for clean multi-line AppleScript in Claude commands
antiPatterns: Granting full disk access when only Mail access is needed
Running scripts without checking mailbox names first (INBOX vs Inbox varies by provider)
Loading full email content when only metadata is needed (significantly slower)

faq.question: Does this skill work with Gmail or Outlook?
faq.answer: No, this skill only works with Apple Mail. Use your email client's built-in automation for other clients.

faq.question: How many emails can I process at once?
faq.answer: AppleScript can handle thousands, but processing many emails with content loading is slow. Limit to 10-50 messages.

faq.question: Can I use this with other AI assistants?
faq.answer: Yes, the AppleScript examples work with any tool that can execute shell commands like osascript.

faq.question: Is my email data sent to external servers?
faq.answer: No. All AppleScript runs locally on your Mac through osascript. Email data never leaves your machine.

faq.question: Why does macOS prompt for permission?
faq.answer: Apple requires explicit user consent for automation and privacy protection. Grant access in System Settings > Privacy.

faq.question: How is this different from Gmail filters?
faq.answer: This provides programmatic control over Apple Mail itself. Gmail filters run on Google's servers.
